events.title: Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes
events.summary: Explosions were heard across Iran overnight, shortly after neighbouring Gulf states began to report attacks.

events.title: New Delhi tells shipowners not to deploy Indian seafarers on Hormuz routes
events.summary: NEW DELHI, July 16 - India has ordered shipowners, ship managers and recruitment companies not to deploy the country's seafarers on vessels undertaking trips through the Strait of Hormuz amid renewed fighting in the region.

events.title: French PM Lecornu arrives in Rabat for talks amid diplomatic reset
events.summary: Another milestone for France-Morocco relations as French Prime Minister Sebastian Lecornu arrived in Rabat on Wednesday.

events.title: Europe Today: Middle East tensions rise as Europe steps up support for Ukraine
events.summary: US strikes on Iran escalate tensions in the Middle East as fears grow over the Strait of Hormuz and global energy markets. In Kyiv, the EU and Ukraine deepen defence cooperation with a new drone partnership, while Europe debates its role in supporting Ukraine’s war effort.

events.title: China’s lab monkey prices hit Covid-era highs on new drug R&D demand
events.summary: A boom in China’s new drug research and development is driving laboratory monkey prices back to coronavirus pandemic-era highs, forcing some researchers to halt their studies. According to Guo Xiangyu, a scientist at Jinan University in Guangzhou whose research focuses on developing non-human primate models for major brain diseases, laboratory monkey prices have risen steadily since Chinese New Year. Suppliers are now generally quoting about 180,000 yuan (US$27,000) per animal. “It is not just a...

events.title: Credit card outage hits convenience stores, other merchants across Japan
events.summary: Major credit card companies are rushing to restore services.

events.title: Azerbaijan at 'real peace' with Armenia but wants it to change constitution
events.summary: SHUSHA, Azerbaijan, July 16 - Azerbaijan and Armenia are at \"real peace\" and rebuilding trade links after decades of conflict, a senior Azerbaijani official told Reuters, but Baku is insisting on changes to Armenia's constitution before a final deal can be signed.

events.title: India bars seafarers from Strait of Hormuz voyages after attacks
events.summary: The IMO has warned that the chokepoint remains too dangerous for commercial vessels at present.

events.title: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang courts Japanese suppliers with pork skewers
events.summary: Huang later emerged to pass red bean cakes to bystanders gathered outside an izakaya.

events.title: Uganda to discharge last Ebola patient, spokesperson says
events.summary: KAMPALA, July 16 - Uganda is due to discharge its last Ebola patient on Thursday, triggering a 42-day countdown that could see the country declared free of the virus, a spokesperson for the government said.

events.title: London vs. Paris vs. Rome: How these major capitals compare for a European city break
events.summary: London, Paris and Rome have been captivating visitors for centuries, but each offers something different. From cultural icons and culinary highlights to transport links and hotel prices, here's how the three European tourism heavyweights compare for a city break.

events.title: ‘I associate them with a day-off’: Tokyo workers encouraged to wear shorts to work to save on AC
events.summary: People in Japan - where many workers are forced to wear suits to work - are divided over whether shorts are appropriate for work, with one saying, “I just don't think hairy legs look very neat”.

events.title: UK proposes voluntary overnight social media curfew for older teens
events.summary: Latest government measure aimed at curbing digital harm comes after under-16 social media ban announced last month.

events.title: Food inflation is finally slowing. So why could your supermarket bill start rising again?
events.summary: Food inflation in Europe is easing, but the recent oil and fertiliser shock could still push up supermarket prices with a delay of more than a year, while this summer's extreme weather is likely to be a bigger driver of eurozone food inflation next year than the Iran war, according to economists.

events.title: South Korea top court upholds jail term for MP over church bribes
events.summary: The ruling stripped Kweon Seong-dong of his parliamentary seat.

events.title: 'Race was used as a weapon': Fiji coup frontman urged to reveal backers
events.summary: Mahendra Chaudhry - a former Fiji prime minister removed from power and held hostage during the 2000 coup - wants George Speight to name those behind the racist takeover.

events.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 12 people, Palestinian authorities say
events.summary: Israeli airstrikes have killed at least a dozen people in Gaza over the past two days, local health officials said Wednesday, as strikes continue almost daily despite a months-old ceasefire with Hamas.

events.title: Study of Botticelli paintings reveals muse behind 'Venus' died of tumour
events.summary: A new study in Endocrinology, Diabetes & Metabolism suggests a diagnosis that could explain the death of Simonetta Vespucci, Botticelli’s muse, using facial recognition technology.

events.title: Argentina defeats England 2-1 in World Cup semi-final with a dramatic late double
events.summary: The defending champions' victory means they will face Spain, who defeated France on Tuesday, in Sunday's final in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

events.title: Actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, agent confirms
events.summary: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.

events.title: Europe needs to catch up with the AI surge in hospitals, WHO says
events.summary: AI solutions are being deployed across European hospitals; however, only a few countries govern them, putting patients at risk, the WHO Europe chief said.

events.title: Interactive map predicts agricultural decline from climate change by end of century
events.summary: A CSIC team in Barcelona has developed CADI, a platform that uses a 10-kilometre grid to estimate how much farmland will lose productivity to climate by 2100; in Spain, inland areas fare worse than the Cantabrian coast.

events.title: Jia Qianqian was a literary high-flier. Then came the online claims of plagiarism
events.summary: Jia Qianqian, daughter of internationally acclaimed writer Jia Pingwa, has become the latest high-profile figure in Chinese academia to fall from grace after a university investigation confirmed allegations of plagiarism. In a statement on Wednesday, Northwest University said its months-long investigation found that nine of Jia’s published papers contained plagiarised material. The university had terminated her teaching appointment and revoked her associate professor title. In addition, her 2009...

events.title: Seoul’s historical royal sites see 6.6% jump in foreign visitor numbers
events.summary: Gyeongbokgung Palace was the most visited site with 3.65 million visitors.

events.title: Senior Chinese delegation visits North Korea for talks
events.summary: China's fourth-highest-ranked official, Wang Huning, held talks with a top official of North Korea's governing party.

events.title: US strikes around Tehran for first time in most recent wave of attacks, says Iranian state media
events.summary: The US says it fired a missile into a Curaçao-flagged oil tanker's funnel after claiming the ship, which was sailing toward Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export terminal in the Persian Gulf, "ignored multiple warnings".

events.title: South Korea’s international adoptees seek justice, not homecoming
events.summary: South Korea's government has acknowledged wrongs, but advocates say accountability is still lacking.

events.title: Over 500 feared dead in two suspected shipwrecks off Myanmar: UN
events.summary: The two vessels departed from war-torn Myanmar’s Rakhine State in late June.

events.title: Doctor who damaged Japan temples, shrines by performing ‘anointings’ admits to own ‘delusion’
events.summary: The US-based man had reportedly performed "anointings" on shrines and temples across Japan since at least 2012.

events.title: Blanche apologises on Capitol Hill for Epstein files 'mistakes'
events.summary: Blanche faces a bipartisan grilling as he seeks to assume the role of US attorney general permanently.

events.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
events.summary: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

events.title: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument
events.summary: The bill would allow assisted dying for terminally ill adults who meet strict criteria.
